# FiguraMC

This wiki is under heavy construction.

To contribute to this wiki, see [Wanted Pages](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Special:WantedPages "Special:WantedPages")
, [Stubs](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Category:Stubs "Category:Stubs")
, and [Contributing](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/FiguraMC:Contributing "FiguraMC:Contributing")
.

# Pings - FiguraMC

**Pings** are a networking feature in Figura that allows an avatar host to communicate additional information to viewers.

The following pages are related to pings:

*   The ping [tutorial](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Pings_(Tutorial) "Pings (Tutorial)")
    
*   [PingAPI](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=PingAPI&action=edit&redlink=1 "PingAPI (page does not exist)")
    , which registers and dispatches pings

Retrieved from "[https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Pings&oldid=260](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Pings&oldid=260)
"

# printJson - FiguraMC

_Aliases: `logJson`_

| Arguments | Returns |
| --- | --- |
| [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)<br>... | [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |

Displays any amount of arguments without separators or the `[lua]` prefix. The return value is the displayed text without the formatting. If an argument is a [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
, it is interpreted as a [JSON text component](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Raw_JSON_text_format)
, or if that fails, the string is displayed directly. All other types are displayed using [`tostring`](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#pdf-tostring)
.

Example
-------

\-- Displays "Hello, world!" in green
printJson \[\[{"text": "Hello, world!", "color": "green"}\]\]
\-- Displays the name of the avatar in gold (example of dynamic text)
printJson(toJson {
    text \= avatar:getName(),
    color \= "gold"
})

See also
--------

*   [toJson](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/ToJson "ToJson")
    
*   [Misode's text component generator](https://misode.github.io/text-component/)
    

Retrieved from "[https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=PrintJson&oldid=642](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=PrintJson&oldid=642)
"

# Print - FiguraMC

Figura's print function is simlar to lua's print function, but has a couple extra features:

*   You can hover over a table to view it's children
    *   This includes userdata such as [Vector3s](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector3 "Vector3")
        

Retrieved from "[https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Print&oldid=814](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Print&oldid=814)
"

---

# EventsAPI - FiguraMC

(Redirected from [Events](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events&redirect=no "Events")
)

Not to be confused with [Event](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Event "Event")
.

Events is a global which contains every [Event](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Event_(type) "Event (type)")
, which are fired when certain things happen. For example, if you want to count ticks, you can use the tick or world tick event.

local tick \= 0

\-- Register event
function events.TICK()
  tick \= tick + 1
end
\-- or
events.TICK:register(function()
  tick \= tick + 1
end)

Note that not everyone needs to count ticks

Methods
-------

| Method | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [getEvents](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Events#getEvents) | Returns a table of every event |

Fields
------

| Event | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [ENTITY\_INIT](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/ENTITY_INIT&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/ENTITY INIT (page does not exist)") | Runs when the player is loaded |
| [TICK](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/TICK&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/TICK (page does not exist)") | Runs every tick the player is rendered |
| [WORLD\_TICK](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/WORLD_TICK&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/WORLD TICK (page does not exist)") | Runs every tick the world is rendered |
| [RENDER](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/RENDER&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/RENDER (page does not exist)") | Runs every frame the player is rendered |
| [POST\_RENDER](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/POST_RENDER&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/POST RENDER (page does not exist)") | Runs every frame AFTER the player is rendered |
| [WORLD\_RENDER](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/WORLD_RENDER&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/WORLD RENDER (page does not exist)") | Runs every frame the world is rendered |
| [POST\_WORLD\_RENDER](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/POST_WORLD_RENDER&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/POST WORLD RENDER (page does not exist)") | Runs every frame AFTER the world is rendered |
| [SKULL\_RENDER](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/SKULL_RENDER&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/SKULL RENDER (page does not exist)") | Runs every frame for every skull when the skull is rendered. |
| [ARROW\_RENDER](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/ARROW_RENDER&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/ARROW RENDER (page does not exist)") | Runs every frame for every arrow when rendered. |
| [TRIDENT\_RENDER](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/TRIDENT_RENDER&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/TRIDENT RENDER (page does not exist)") | Runs every frame for every trident when rendered. |
| [ITEM\_RENDER](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/ITEM_RENDER&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/ITEM RENDER (page does not exist)") | Runs every frame for every item when rendered. |
| [CHAT\_SEND\_MESSAGE](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/CHAT_SEND_MESSAGE&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/CHAT SEND MESSAGE (page does not exist)") | Runs every time the player sends something in chat, including commands. Allows for changing the message |
| [CHAT\_RECEIVE\_MESSAGE](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/CHAT_RECEIVE_MESSAGE&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/CHAT RECEIVE MESSAGE (page does not exist)") | Runs every time the player receives something in chat. Allows for changing the message |
| [MOUSE\_SCROLL](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/MOUSE_SCROLL&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/MOUSE SCROLL (page does not exist)") | Runs every time the mouse is scrolled. Allows for cancelling the vanilla function |
| [MOUSE\_MOVE](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/MOUSE_MOVE&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/MOUSE MOVE (page does not exist)") | Runs every time the mouse is moved. Allows for cancelling the vanilla function |
| [MOUSE\_PRESS](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/MOUSE_PRESS&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/MOUSE PRESS (page does not exist)") | Runs every time the mouse is clicked. Allows for cancelling the vanilla function |
| [KEY\_PRESS](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/KEY_PRESS&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/KEY PRESS (page does not exist)") | Runs every time a key is pressed. Allows for cancelling the vanilla function |
| [CHAR\_TYPED](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/CHAR_TYPED&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/CHAR TYPED (page does not exist)") | Runs every time a character is typed. |
| [USE\_ITEM](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/USE_ITEM&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/USE ITEM (page does not exist)") | Runs every time an item is used by the player. |
| [ON\_PLAY\_SOUND](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/ON_PLAY_SOUND&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/ON PLAY SOUND (page does not exist)") | Runs every time a sound is played |
| [RESOURCE\_RELOAD](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/RESOURCE_RELOAD&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/RESOURCE RELOAD (page does not exist)") | Runs every time resources are reloaded |
| [TOTEM](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/TOTEM&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/TOTEM (page does not exist)") | Runs every time a totem of undying is activated |
| [DAMAGE](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/DAMAGE&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/DAMAGE (page does not exist)") | Runs whenever the player takes damage |

Methods
-------

#### getEvents

* * *

Returns a table of every event

\-- Example function to clear all events
function clearEvents()
  for \_, v in pairs(events:getEvents()) do
    v:clear()
  end
end

Retrieved from "[https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=EventsAPI&oldid=569](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=EventsAPI&oldid=569)
"

# EventsAPI - FiguraMC

Not to be confused with [Event](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Event "Event")
.

Events is a global which contains every [Event](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Event_(type) "Event (type)")
, which are fired when certain things happen. For example, if you want to count ticks, you can use the tick or world tick event.

local tick \= 0

\-- Register event
function events.TICK()
  tick \= tick + 1
end
\-- or
events.TICK:register(function()
  tick \= tick + 1
end)

Note that not everyone needs to count ticks

Methods
-------

| Method | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [getEvents](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/EventsAPI#getEvents) | Returns a table of every event |

------

| --- | --- |

| [SKULL\_RENDER](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/SKULL_RENDER&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/SKULL RENDER (page does not exist)") | Runs every frame for every skull when the skull is rendered. |
| [ARROW\_RENDER](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/ARROW_RENDER&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/ARROW RENDER (page does not exist)") | Runs every frame for every arrow when rendered. |

| [CHAT\_SEND\_MESSAGE](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/CHAT_SEND_MESSAGE&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/CHAT SEND MESSAGE (page does not exist)") | Runs every time the player sends something in chat, including commands. Allows for changing the message |
| [CHAT\_RECEIVE\_MESSAGE](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/CHAT_RECEIVE_MESSAGE&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/CHAT RECEIVE MESSAGE (page does not exist)") | Runs every time the player receives something in chat. Allows for changing the message |
| [MOUSE\_SCROLL](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/MOUSE_SCROLL&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/MOUSE SCROLL (page does not exist)") | Runs every time the mouse is scrolled. Allows for cancelling the vanilla function |
| [MOUSE\_MOVE](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/MOUSE_MOVE&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/MOUSE MOVE (page does not exist)") | Runs every time the mouse is moved. Allows for cancelling the vanilla function |
| [MOUSE\_PRESS](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/MOUSE_PRESS&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/MOUSE PRESS (page does not exist)") | Runs every time the mouse is clicked. Allows for cancelling the vanilla function |
| [KEY\_PRESS](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/KEY_PRESS&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/KEY PRESS (page does not exist)") | Runs every time a key is pressed. Allows for cancelling the vanilla function |
| [CHAR\_TYPED](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/CHAR_TYPED&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/CHAR TYPED (page does not exist)") | Runs every time a character is typed. |
| [USE\_ITEM](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/USE_ITEM&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/USE ITEM (page does not exist)") | Runs every time an item is used by the player. |
| [ON\_PLAY\_SOUND](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/ON_PLAY_SOUND&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/ON PLAY SOUND (page does not exist)") | Runs every time a sound is played |
| [RESOURCE\_RELOAD](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/RESOURCE_RELOAD&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/RESOURCE RELOAD (page does not exist)") | Runs every time resources are reloaded |
| [TOTEM](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/TOTEM&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/TOTEM (page does not exist)") | Runs every time a totem of undying is activated |
| [DAMAGE](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/DAMAGE&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/DAMAGE (page does not exist)") | Runs whenever the player takes damage |

Methods
-------

#### getEvents

* * *

Returns a table of every event

\-- Example function to clear all events
function clearEvents()
  for \_, v in pairs(events:getEvents()) do
    v:clear()
  end
end

Retrieved from "[https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=EventsAPI&oldid=569](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=EventsAPI&oldid=569)
"

---

# ConfigAPI - FiguraMC

This API is only available on the host.

For more information, see [Pings](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Pings "Pings")
.

The `ConfigAPI` allows storing and loading data between reloads of your avatar.

ConfigAPI is available as the global `config` variable.

Data types
----------

Only some data types are supported by ConfigAPI. All other data types will be converted to [nil](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
. The supported data types are:

*   [nil](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
    
*   [boolean](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
    
*   [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
    
*   [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
    
*   [table](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
    
*   all types of [Vector](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector "Vector")
    
*   all types of [Matrix](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vectors_and_Matrices "Vectors and Matrices")
    

ConfigAPI methods
-----------------

### Configuration switching

#### setName

* * *

Also available as `ConfigAPI:name`.

Sets the name of the configuration file to read and write data to. By default, the name of the configuration file is the same as the name of the currently loaded avatar.

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `setName(name [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) )` | self ConfigAPI |

config:setName("my\_config\_file\_name")

#### getName

* * *

Returns the name of the active configuration file.

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `getName()` | [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |

\-- Print the active configuration file's name.
print(config:getName())

### Reading and writing data

#### load

* * *

Read a saved value from the active configuration file (see [setName](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/ConfigAPI#setName)
) by key and return it. If no key is given, return all of the saved keys and values in a table.

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `load(key [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) )` | [any](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)<br> \| [nil](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |
| `load()` | [table](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |

\-- Print all saved values.
printTable(config:load())
\-- Print one specific value.
print(config:load("my\_value"))

#### save

* * *

Associate a key with a value in the active configuration file (see [setName](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/ConfigAPI#setName)
.) Only some types of values are allowed; see [Data Types](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/ConfigAPI#Data_Types)
 for details.

If [nil](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
 is provided as the value, then the key is removed from the file.

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `save(key [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) , value [any](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)  \| [nil](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) )` | self ConfigAPI |

\-- Save a value to be used later.
config:save("my\_value", "Figura Wiki")

See also
--------

*   [Persisting Variables](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Tutorials/Persisting-Variables "Tutorials/Persisting-Variables")
     (tutorial)

# Vectors and Matrices - FiguraMC

(Redirected from [MatricesAPI](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=MatricesAPI&redirect=no "MatricesAPI")
)

This page is a [stub](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "wikipedia:Wikipedia:Stub")
. You can help this wiki by [expanding it](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Vectors_and_Matrices&action=edit)

Not to be confused with [Vectors (Global)](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Vectors_(Global)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Vectors (Global) (page does not exist)")
, [Vector](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector "Vector")
, or [Matrices (Global)](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Matrices_(Global)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Matrices (Global) (page does not exist)")
.

Vectors and matrices at their core are **a set of numbers** that represent something (e.g. a Vector3 could represent a position in the world.)

Vectors are one-dimensional; for example, a [Vector3](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector3 "Vector3")
 holds 3 numbers. Vectors are most commonly used for representing the position and rotation of things.

Matrices are two-dimensional grids. All matrices are square, so a Matrix3 is a 3 by 3 grid of numbers. Matrices are most commonly used to represent transformations of vectors. For more in-depth information, see [the Wikipedia article on transformation matrices](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Transformation_matrix "wikipedia:Transformation matrix")
.

Vectors
-------

Many functions that take in a vector will also take in the same amount of numbers and internally convert it into a Vector.

Vectors come in 3 types: Vector2, Vector3, and Vector4. The number refers to the size of the vector (Vector4 has 4 numbers, etc.) You can create a vector by using the global method `vec` as shown:

*   vec(x [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
    , y [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
    , z [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
     | [nil](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
    , w [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
     | [nil](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
    )

This method will return a vector based on how many arguments you input.

Matrices
--------

Matrices are a set of vectors, which are normally used to change where models are, how they look, etc.

Contents

---

# Vectors and Matrices - FiguraMC

(Redirected from [VectorsAPI](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=VectorsAPI&redirect=no "VectorsAPI")
)

This page is a [stub](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "wikipedia:Wikipedia:Stub")
. You can help this wiki by [expanding it](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Vectors_and_Matrices&action=edit)

Not to be confused with [Vectors (Global)](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Vectors_(Global)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Vectors (Global) (page does not exist)")
, [Vector](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector "Vector")
, or [Matrices (Global)](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Matrices_(Global)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Matrices (Global) (page does not exist)")
.

Vectors and matrices at their core are **a set of numbers** that represent something (e.g. a Vector3 could represent a position in the world.)

Vectors are one-dimensional; for example, a [Vector3](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector3 "Vector3")
 holds 3 numbers. Vectors are most commonly used for representing the position and rotation of things.

Matrices are two-dimensional grids. All matrices are square, so a Matrix3 is a 3 by 3 grid of numbers. Matrices are most commonly used to represent transformations of vectors. For more in-depth information, see [the Wikipedia article on transformation matrices](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Transformation_matrix "wikipedia:Transformation matrix")
.

Vectors
-------

Many functions that take in a vector will also take in the same amount of numbers and internally convert it into a Vector.

Vectors come in 3 types: Vector2, Vector3, and Vector4. The number refers to the size of the vector (Vector4 has 4 numbers, etc.) You can create a vector by using the global method `vec` as shown:

*   vec(x [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
    , y [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
    , z [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
     | [nil](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
    , w [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
     | [nil](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
    )

This method will return a vector based on how many arguments you input.

Matrices
--------

Matrices are a set of vectors, which are normally used to change where models are, how they look, etc.

# Event (type) - FiguraMC

(Redirected from [Event](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Event&redirect=no "Event")
)

Not to be confused with [Events](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Events "Events")
.

The event type is used for events such as `events.TICK`. To create an event function, use one of the following.

 local tick \= 0
function events.WORLD\_TICK()
  tick \= tick + 1
end
\-- OR
local tick \= 0
events.WORLD\_TICK:register(function()
  tick \= tick + 1
end, "TICK\_COUNTER")

Methods
-------

| Method | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [remove](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Event#remove) | Takes in a [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)<br> with the name of the event, and removes it, stopping it from being called |
| [clear](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Event#clear) | Clears the event of all methods |
| [register](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Event#register) | Takes in a [function](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)<br> to run when the event is fired, with an optional name argument ([string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)<br>) |
| [getRegisteredCount](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Event#getRegisteredCount) | Returns the amount of functions registered, with an optional name argument ([string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)<br>) |

#### remove

* * *

Removes a function from an event, returning how many were removed

Overloads:

| Arguments | Return |
| --- | --- |
| name: [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) | [integer](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |
| function: [function](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) | [integer](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |

local tick \= 0
function tickThing()
  tick \= tick + 1
  print("tick " .. tick)
  if tick \> 5 then
    events.TICK:remove("thing:)
  end
end
events.TICK:register(tickThing, "tickThing")

#### clear

* * *

Similar to remove, but removes every event

Overloads:

| Arguments | Return |
| --- | --- |
| none | [nil](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |

\-- Example function to clear all events
function clearEvents()
  for \_, v in pairs(events:getEvents()) do
    v:clear()
  end
end

#### register

Registers a function to an event

Overloads:

| Arguments | Return |
| --- | --- |
| func: [function](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)<br>, name: [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) | Event |
| func: [function](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) | Event |

local fps \= 0
events.RENDER:register(function()
  fps \= client.getFPS()
end)

#### getRegisteredCount

Gets the number of registered functions in an event, optionally filtering by name

Overloads:

| Arguments | Return |
| --- | --- |
| name: [Integer](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) | Event |
| none | [Integer](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |

local fps \= 0
events.RENDER:register(function()
  fps \= client.getFPS()
end)

Retrieved from "[https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Event\_(type)&oldid=527](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Event_(type)&oldid=527)
"

# ParentType - FiguraMC

(Redirected from [ParentTypes](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=ParentTypes&redirect=no "ParentTypes")
)

**ParentType**s dictate how Figura positions and renders parts in your model. For example, some (like [Head](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/ParentTypes#Head)
 or [LeftLeg](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/ParentTypes#LeftLeg)
) cause the part to mimic the motions of a part of the standard Minecraft player model. Others (like [LeftItemPivot](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/ParentTypes#LeftItemPivot)
) control the positioning of special parts like the player's currently held item, armor, and parrots. Finally, ParentTypes like [World](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/ParentTypes#World)
 change the way the part is positioned entirely. Each ParentType has one or more names associated with them.

In Blockbench
-------------

In Blockbench, **groups** (and only groups, not cubes or meshes) can be assigned ParentTypes by starting the name of the group with one of the names below.

ⓘ Note

Keywords are **case-sensitive**. This means that changing the capitalization might make a keyword non-functional.

For example, all of the following group names will assign the ParentType of [Head](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/ParentTypes#Head)
, which will cause the part to move and rotate like the vanilla player's head.

*   Head
*   HEAD
*   Head2
*   Headphones (be careful of assigning ParentTypes by accident!)

The following will **not** assign the ParentType of [Head](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/ParentTypes#Head)
:

*   head ('head' isn't a ParentType name; capitalization matters)
*   BigHead (doesn't start with the ParentType)
*   HeAd ('HeAd' isn't a ParentType name)

ⓘ Warning

Watch out for English words that incidentally start with ParentType names, like **Gui**tar, which assigns a ParentType of [Hud](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/ParentTypes#Hud)
 (through its alias 'Gui'!)

Via Scripting
-------------

Calling [setParentType](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/ModelPart#setParentType "ModelPart")
 on any [ModelPart](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/ModelPart "ModelPart")
 (including cubes and meshes) will override its ParentType.

Simply [access](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/ModelPart#Accessing_ModelParts "ModelPart")
 the ModelPart and call [setParentType](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/ModelPart#setParentType "ModelPart")
 on it, passing the ParentType as a [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
. For example:

local head \= models.model.root.Head
head:setParentType("RightArm") \-- make the part act like the vanilla player's right arm

To remove the ParentType from a ModelPart, use `"None"`:

local head \= models.model.root.Head
head:setParentType("None") \-- remove the ParentType

ModelParts don't remember their original ParentType! If you need to reference the original ParentType of a ModelPart, get ([getParentType](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/ModelPart#getParentType "ModelPart")
) and store it in a variable and use it later:

local head \= models.model.root.Head
local headParentType \= head:getParentType() \-- store the current ParentType for later
head:setParentType("None") \-- clear the ParentType

List of ParentTypes
-------------------

### Vanilla model mimicry

#### None

* * *

_Aliases: `NONE`, `Model`, `MODEL`_

No special properties\[_verify_\]. The default behavior.

#### Head

* * *

_Aliases: `HEAD`_

Mimics the transformations of the vanilla player's head. Additionally, if there are no [Skull](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/ParentTypes#Skull)
 [ModelParts](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/ModelPart "ModelPart")
, Head [ModelParts](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/ModelPart "ModelPart")
 will be used to replace the vanilla skull\[_verify_\].

#### Body

* * *

_Aliases: `BODY`_

Mimics the transformations of the vanilla player's body (torso.)

#### RightArm

* * *

_Aliases: `RIGHT_ARM`_

Mimics the transformations of the vanilla player's right arm. Parts with this ParentType will be rendered in first-person view if the vanilla player's right arm would be visible (e.g. not holding any item.)

#### LeftArm

* * *

_Aliases: `LEFT_ARM`_

Mimics the transformations of the vanilla player's right arm. Parts with this ParentType will be rendered in first-person view if the vanilla player's left arm would be visible (e.g. not holding any item, and left-handed.)

#### RightLeg

* * *

_Aliases: `RIGHT_LEG`_

Mimics the transformations of the vanilla player's right leg.

#### LeftLeg

* * *

_Aliases: `LEFT_LEG`_

Mimics the transformations of the vanilla player's left leg.

#### RightElytra

* * *

_Aliases: `RIGHT_ELYTRA`, `RightElytron`, `RIGHT_ELYTRON`_

Mimics the transformations of the vanilla player's right Elytra wing. Only rendered when the player has an Elytra equipped.

#### LeftElytra

* * *

_Aliases: `LEFT_ELYTRA`, `LeftElytron`, `LEFT_ELYTRON`_

Mimics the transformations of the vanilla player's left Elytra wing. Only rendered when the player has an Elytra equipped.

#### Cape

* * *

_Aliases: `CAPE`_

Mimics the transformations of the vanilla player's cape, even if the player doesn't own a cape\[_verify_\].

### Special rendering modes

These ParentTypes provide access to rendering functionality that might not be otherwise possible.

#### World

* * *

_Aliases: `WORLD`_

The ModelPart will be attached to the world, instead of your player.

World parts' [position](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/ModelPart#getPos "ModelPart")
 reflect their X/Y/Z coordinates in the world, relative to (0, 0, 0). 1 block is equivalent to 16 ModelPart units; to convert between world coordinates and ModelPart coordinates, scale each axis by a factor of 16 (or 1⁄16.)

A World ModelPart with [rotation](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/ModelPart#getRot "ModelPart")
 (0, 0, 0) faces _south_ in the world, which is the opposite of Blockbench's North indicator.

World ModelParts are always visible in first-person view.

#### Hud

* * *

_Aliases: `HUD`, `HeadsUpDisplay`, `Gui`, `GUI`, `GraphicalUserInterface`, `JraficalUserInterface`_

The ModelPart will be rendered on your HUD, instead of in the world or attached to your model. Only the avatar owner and players spectating the owner, will be able to see the HUD ModelParts.

The top-left of the screen is (0, 0, 0); 1 ModelPart unit is equivalent to 1 GUI unit, which scales with the GUI Scale Minecraft option.

Decreasing the X coordinate (-X) will move the ModelPart to the right, and decreasing the Y coordinate (-Y) will move the ModelPart down. The Z axis determines the rendering order of parts, with lower Z values (including negative) rendering closer to the screen.

#### Camera

* * *

_Aliases: `CAMERA`, `Billboard`, `BILLBOARD`_

The ModelPart will always face the camera, like nameplates or particles in vanilla. This rendering mode is also called 'Billboard rendering.'

#### Skull

* * *

_Aliases: `SKULL`, `☠`_

The ModelPart will replace the vanilla Skull (placed player head blocks.)

#### Portrait

* * *

_Aliases: `PORTRAIT`_

The ModelPart will be used to render the player icon in the player list (also called the TAB list.)

(0, 0, 0) is the bottom center point on the icon, and the rendering area extends 4 units on the X axis in each direction (-4 to +4) and 8 units on the Y axis (0 to 8.)

#### Arrow

* * *

_Aliases: `ARROW`_

The ModelPart will be used to replace arrows that are fired by the avatar owner. The ModelPart's pivot point has no effect on this ParentType. (0, 0, 0) is the point on the vanilla arrow's shaft just before the head of the arrow.

#### Trident

* * *

_Aliases: `TRIDENT`_

The ModelPart will be used to replace tridents that are thrown by the avatar owner. The ModelPart's pivot point has no effect on this ParentType.

#### Item

* * *

_Aliases: `ITEM`_

This ParentType designates a custom item model that can be used with the [`ITEM_RENDER` event](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Events/ITEM_RENDER&action=edit&redlink=1 "Events/ITEM RENDER (page does not exist)")
 to display a custom item.

This page is not complete! TODO: add pivots

Retrieved from "[https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=ParentType&oldid=645](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=ParentType&oldid=645)
"

# FileAPI - FiguraMC

This API is only available on the host.

For more information, see [Pings](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Pings "Pings")
.

`FileAPI` allows your avatar to access files in isolated folder.

FileAPI is available as global `file` variable.

Security
--------

FileAPI is fully isolated in your data folder. It is located in your Figura folder, so, default path for it would be `figura/data`. FileAPI can't access any files or directories out of this folder, even when using symbolic links. Attempt to access file or directory outside data folder will result in error. Every used path in methods is relative to data folder.

Methods
-------

| Method | Brief Description |
| --- | --- |
| [allowed](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/FileAPI#allowed) | Checks if FileAPI usage is allowed |
| [isPathAllowed](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/FileAPI#isPathAllowed) | Checks if a path is allowed for usage |
| [exists](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/FileAPI#exists) | Checks if a path exists |
| [delete](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/FileAPI#delete) | Deletes a file or directory, returning whether it was successful |
| [isDirectory](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/FileAPI#isDirectory) | Checks if a path is a directory |
| [mkdir](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/FileAPI#mkdir) | Creates a directory |
| [mkdirs](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/FileAPI#mkdirs) | Creates a directory and all parent directories |
| [list](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/FileAPI#list) | Lists files and directories contained under a path |
| [isFile](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/FileAPI#isFile) | Checks if a path is a file |
| [openReadStream](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/FileAPI#openReadStream) | Opens an input stream for a file |
| [openWriteStream](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/FileAPI#openWriteStream) | Opens an output stream for a file |
| [readString](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/FileAPI#readString) | Reads a file and returns its contents as a string |
| [writeString](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/FileAPI#writeString) | Writes a file's contents as a string |

### Security checks

Security related methods of FileAPI.

#### allowed

* * *

Checks if FileAPI usage is allowed for this avatar.

Overloads:

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `allowed()` | [boolean](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |

\-- Prints false on non-host avatar.
print(file:allowed())

#### isPathAllowed

* * *

Checks if path is allowed for usage.

Overloads:

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `isPathAllowed(path [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) )` | [boolean](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |

\-- Relative paths are always relative to data folder.
print(file:isPathAllowed("foo")) \-- true
\-- Relative paths that are pointing outside of data folder are not allowed.
print(file:isPathAllowed("../foo")) \-- false
\-- Absolute paths pointing to figura data folder are also allowed.
print(file:isPathAllowed("C:/path/to/figura/data/foo")) \-- true
\-- Absolute paths outside of data folder are not allowed.
print(file:isPathAllowed("C:/foo")) \-- false

### Common

Methods related both to files and directories.

#### exists

* * *

Checks if specified path exists.

Overloads:

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `exists(path [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) )` | [boolean](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |

print(file:exists("foo.bar")) \-- true

#### delete

* * *

Deletes file or directory by specified path. Returns true if successful.

Overloads:

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `delete(path [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) )` | [boolean](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |

\-- Directory fooBar is empty
print(file:delete("fooBar")) \-- true
\-- Directory barFoo has files inside
print(file:delete("barFoo")) \-- false
\-- foo.bar is not open by any program
print(file:delete("foo.bar")) \-- true
\-- bar.foo doesn't exist
print(file:delete("bar.foo")) \-- false

### Directories

Directory related methods of FileAPI.

#### isDirectory

* * *

Checks if specified path is directory.

Overloads:

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `isDirectory(path [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) )` | [boolean](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |

print(file:isDirectory("fooBar")) \-- true

#### mkdir

* * *

Creates directory by specified path. Returns true if successful.

Overloads:

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `mkdir(path [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) )` | [boolean](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |

print(file:exists("foo")) \-- false
print(file:mkdir("foo")) \-- true
print(file:exists("foo")) \-- true

**@see** [exists](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/FileAPI#exists)

#### mkdirs

* * *

Creates directory by specified path, and all parent directories if they doesn't exist. Returns true if successful.

Overloads:

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `mkdir(path [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) )` | [boolean](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |

print(file:exists("foo/bar")) \-- false
print(file:mkdir("foo/bar")) \-- false
print(file:mkdirs("foo/bar")) \-- true
print(file:exists("foo/bar")) \-- true

**@see** [exists](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/FileAPI#exists)
, [mkdir](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/FileAPI#mkdir)

#### list

* * *

Lists files and directories by specified path. Returns table with paths, or null if path doesn't exist or not a directory.

Overloads:

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `list(path [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) )` | [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)<br>\[\] \| [nil](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |

printTable(file:list("")) \--\[\[{\
"foo.bar",\
"fooBar",\
"barFoo"\
}\]\]

  

### Files

Methods related to files.

#### isFile

* * *

Checks if specified path is a file.

Overloads:

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `isFile(path [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) )` | [boolean](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |

print(file:isFile("foo.bar")) \-- true

#### openReadStream

* * *

Open input stream for file at specified path. Throws an error if file doesn't exist.

Overloads:

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `openReadStream(path [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) )` | [InputStream](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=InputStream&action=edit&redlink=1 "InputStream (page does not exist)") |

\-- Opens an input stream.
local is \= file:openReadStream("foo.bar")

\-- Stream must be closed when you finished working with it.
is:close()

**@see** [InputStream:close](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=InputStream&action=edit&redlink=1 "InputStream (page does not exist)")

  

#### openWriteStream

* * *

Open output stream for file at specified path. Throws an error if unable to create a file.

Overloads:

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `openWriteStream(path [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) )` | [OutputStream](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=OutputStream&action=edit&redlink=1 "OutputStream (page does not exist)") |

\-- Opens an output stream.
local os \= file:openWriteStream("bar.foo")

\-- Stream must be closed when you finished working with it.
os:close()

  
**@see** [OutputStream:close](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=OutputStream&action=edit&redlink=1 "OutputStream (page does not exist)")

#### readString

* * *

Reads file at specified path and returns it's contents as a string in specified encoding. Default encoding is `utf8`.

Overloads:

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `readString(path [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) )` | [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |
| `readString(path [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) , encoding [BufferEncoding](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/BufferEncoding "BufferEncoding") )` | [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |

print(file:readString("foo.bar")) \-- Hello, world!

#### writeString

* * *

Writes string in specified encoding to file by specified path. Default encoding is `utf8`.

Overloads:

| Arguments |
| --- |
| `writeString(path [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) , contents [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) )` |
| `writeString(path [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) , contents [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) , encoding [BufferEncoding](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/BufferEncoding "BufferEncoding") )` |

print(file:writeString("foo.bar", "Hello, world!"))

Contents

---

# Vector2 - FiguraMC

For general information on vectors, see [Vector](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector "Vector")
 and [Vectors and Matrices](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vectors_and_Matrices "Vectors and Matrices")
.

A **Vector2** is a [vector](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector "Vector")
 that contains 2 numbers.

Methods
-------

| Method | Brief description |
| --- | --- |
| [length](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#length) | Computes the length of the vector. |
| [lengthSquared](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#lengthSquared) | Computes the squared length of the vector. |
| [dot](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#dot) | Computes the dot product between vectors. |
| [add](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#add)<br> _in-place_ | Adds vectors. |
| [subtract](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#subtract)<br> _in-place_ | Subtracts vectors. |
| [offset](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#offset)<br> _in-place_ | Adds a number to each component of the vector. |
| [multiply](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#multiply)<br> _in-place_ | Performs component multiplication. |
| [transform](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#transform)<br> _in-place_ | Transforms a vector with a matrix. |
| [divide](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#divide)<br> _in-place_ | Performs component division. |
| [reduce](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#reduce)<br> _in-place_ | Runs the modulo operation on each component of the vector. |
| [scale](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#scale)<br> _in-place_ | Multiplies each component of the vector by a number. |
| [normalize](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#normalize)<br> _in-place_ | Normalizes the vector, making its length 1. |
| [clampLength](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#clampLength)<br> _in-place_ | Applies bounds to the length of the vector, scaling as needed. |
| [clamped](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#clamped) | Returns a new copy of this vector with [clampLength](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#clampLength)<br> applied. |
| [normalized](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#normalized) | Returns a new, normalized copy of the vector. |
| [toRad](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#toRad) | Returns a copy of this vector, converted to radians. |
| [toDeg](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#toDeg) | Returns a copy of this vector, converted to degrees. |
| [floor](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#floor)<br> _in-place_ | Floors the components of the vector. |
| [ceil](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#ceil)<br> _in-place_ | Ceilings the components of the vector. |
| [copy](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#copy) | Returns a copy of this vector. |
| [set](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#set)<br> _in-place_ | Assigns the values from another vector to this one. |
| [reset](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#reset)<br> _in-place_ | Sets the components of this vector to 0. |
| [applyFunc](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#applyFunc)<br> _in-place_ | Apply a function to the components of this vector. |
| [augmented](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#augmented) | Convert this vector into a [Vector3](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector3 "Vector3")<br> by appending a number. |

Fields
------

| Field | Brief description |
| --- | --- |
| [\_](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#field_zero) | Always 0. |
| [x](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#field_x)<br>, [r](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#field_r)<br>, `[1]` | First component. |
| [y](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#field_y)<br>, [g](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#field_g)<br>, `[2]` | Second component. |

Operators
---------

| Operator | Brief description |
| --- | --- |
| [(vector + vector)](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#meta_add_vv) | Add two vectors. |
| [(vector + number) _comm._](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#meta_add_vn) | Add the number to each component of the vector. |
| [(-vector)](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#meta_unm) | Negate the vector. |
| [(vector - vector)](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#meta_sub_vv) | Subtract two vectors. |
| [(vector - number)](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#meta_sub_vn) | Subtract the number from each component of the vector. |
| [(number - vector)](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#meta_sub_nv) | Negate the vector, then add the number to each component. |
| [(vector \* vector)](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#meta_mul_vv) | Multiply (component-wise) two vectors. |
| [(vector \* number) _comm._](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#meta_mul_vn) | Scale the vector. |
| [(vector \* matrix)](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#meta_mul_vm) | Transform the vector using the matrix. |
| [(vector / vector)](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#meta_div_vv) | Divide (component-wise) two vectors. |
| [(vector / number)](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#meta_div_vn) | Scale the vector by (1/number). |
| [(#vector)](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#meta_len) | Number of components in the vector. |
| [(vector < vector)](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#meta_lt) | Component-wise less than. |
| [(vector <= vector)](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#meta_le) | Component-wise less than or equal. |
| [tostring(vector)](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#meta_tostring) | Result of `tostring(vector)`. |

Methods
-------

### Math operations

Mathematical operations that apply to all vectors, such as computing their length

#### length

* * *

Mathematical definition

Computes the [Euclidean norm](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Norm_(mathematics)#Euclidean_norm "wikipedia:Norm (mathematics)")
 of the vector.

Computes the length of a vector.

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `length()` | [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |

#### lengthSquared

* * *

Computes the length of a vector, squared. Due to the nature of the length calculation, this is a little bit faster than [`length`](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#length)
.

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `lengthSquared()` | [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |

#### dot

* * *

Computes the [dot product](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/dot_product "wikipedia:dot product")
 between two vectors. The argument must be the same type as the target vector.

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `dot(Vector2 other)` | [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |

#### add

* * *

Mathematical definition

Performs [vector addition](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Euclidean_vector#Addition_and_subtraction "wikipedia:Euclidean vector")
 with another vector, storing the result in this vector.

Adds the values from this vector and another vector of the same size, and writes them to this vector. To create a new instance from the sum, see [\+ (operator)](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#meta_add_vv)
.

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `add(Vector2 other)` | self Vector2 |

#### subtract

* * *

Mathematical definition

Performs [vector subtraction](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Euclidean_vector#Addition_and_subtraction "wikipedia:Euclidean vector")
 with another vector (in the order **this - other**), storing the result in this vector.

Subtracts the passed vector of the same size from this vector, and writes the result to this vector. To create a new instance from the difference, see [\- (operator)](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#meta_sub_vv)
.

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `subtract(Vector2 other)` | self Vector2 |

#### offset

* * *

Adds _factor_ to all components of this vector. To create a new instance from the result, see [\+ (operator)](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#meta_add_vn)
 or [\- (operator)](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#meta_sub_vn)
.

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `offset([number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)  factor)` | self Vector2 |

#### multiply

* * *

Multiplies vectors of the same size by multiplying their components. The result is written to this vector.

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `multiply(Vector2 other)` | self Vector2 |

#### transform

* * *

Mathematical definition

[Multiplies](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Matrix_multiplication#Matrix_times_vector "wikipedia:Matrix multiplication")
 the provided matrix with this vector, in the order _M \* V_, and writes the resulting vector to this vector.

Applies a matrix transformation to this vector. Equivalent to multiplying the matrix by the vector. The matrix must be the same size as the vector{{#ifVector2|.|; the following are valid:}}

  

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `transform([Matrix2](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vectors_and_Matrices "Vectors and Matrices")  mat)` | self Vector2 |

#### divide

* * *

Divides vectors of the same size by dividing their components. The result is written to this vector.

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `divide(Vector2 other)` | self Vector2 |

#### reduce

* * *

For each component in this vector, perform the [positive modulo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/modulo "wikipedia:modulo")
 operation on it and the equivalent component in the other vector.

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `reduce(Vector2 other)` | self Vector2 |

#### scale

* * *

Multiplies each component in this vector by _factor_.

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `scale([number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)  factor)` | self Vector2 |

#### normalize

* * *

[Normalizes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Unit_vector "wikipedia:Unit vector")
 this vector in-place. After this operation, the [length](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#length)
 of the vector will be 1, **unless the length is currently 0**. If this vector's length is 0, then no normalization will occur.

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `normalize()` | self Vector2 |

#### clampLength

* * *

[Scales](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#scale)
 this vector such that its [length](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#length)
 is within the bounds specified by _minLength_ and _maxLength_. **If this vector's length is 0**, no scaling will be performed, even if _minLength_ is greater than 0. If this vector's length is already within the specified bounds, no scaling will be performed.

Both _minLength_ and _maxLength_ can be omitted or set to [nil](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
, resulting in no lower or upper bound on the length of the vector.

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `clampLength([number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)  \| [nil](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)  minLength, [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)  \| [nil](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)  maxLength)` | self Vector2 |

#### clamped

* * *

[Copies](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#copy)
 this vector, then clamps its length. See [clampLength](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#clampLength)
 for details.

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `clamped([number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)  \| [nil](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)  minLength, [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)  \| [nil](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)  maxLength)` | new Vector2 |

#### normalized

* * *

[Copies](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#copy)
 this vector, then normalizes it. See [normalize](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#normalize)
 for details.

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `normalized()` | new Vector2 |

#### toRad

* * *

Returns a [copy](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#copy)
 of this vector, [scaled](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#scale)
 by a factor of _(pi / 180)_. If this vector represents a rotation in degrees, the result will be a rotation in radians.

_See also [toDeg](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#toDeg)
._

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `toRad()` | new Vector2 |

#### toDeg

* * *

Returns a [copy](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#copy)
 of this vector, [scaled](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#scale)
 by a factor of _(180 / pi)_. If this vector represents a rotation in radians, the result will be a rotation in degrees.

_See also [toRad](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#toRad)
._

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `toDeg()` | new Vector2 |

#### floor

* * *

Returns a [copy](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#copy)
 of this vector with every component floored.

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `floor()` | new Vector2 |

  

#### ceil

* * *

Returns a [copy](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#copy)
 of this vector with every component ceiling-ed.

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `ceil()` | new Vector2 |

### Utility methods

#### copy

* * *

Creates a copy of this vector. Creating a copy will make a new Vector with the same size and values, but which is disconnected from the original. This means that methods like [set](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#set)
 or [add](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#add)
 which modify the vector they are called with will not modify copies.

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `copy()` | new Vector2 |

#### set

* * *

Assigns the values from another vector of the same size to this vector in place. To create a new instance, see [copy](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#copy)
.

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `set(Vector2 other)` | self Vector2 |

#### reset

* * *

Sets all the components of this vector to 0.

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `reset()` | self Vector2 |

#### applyFunc

* * *

Calls the provided function _f_ on every component, and sets this vector's components to the return values of the function.

The function receives the following values as arguments: first, the value as a [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
, then the index ([integer](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
, 1 is x, 2 is y, etc.) The function is expected to return a [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
.

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `applyFunc([function](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)  f)` | self Vector2 |

local A \= vec(1, 2, 3)
\-- cube the values
A:applyFunc(function(value, index) return value\*value\*value end)
print(A) \-- {1, 8, 27}

### Vector2 methods

#### augmented

* * *

Converts this Vector2 into a [Vector3](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector3 "Vector3")
 using the provided number as the third component.

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `augmented([number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)  d)` | [Vector3](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector3 "Vector3") |

Fields
------

### Swizzling

Swizzling is available for all vector types. For details, see [Swizzling](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector#Swizzling "Vector")
.

### `_`

Read-only [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
. Always 0. Attempting to write to this field will have no effect.

### Vector2 fields

#### x, r, 1

* * *

Read/write [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
. The first component of the vector.

#### y, g, 2

* * *

Read/write [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
. The second component of the vector.

Operators
---------

Lua operators, such as `+`, `-`, and `*`, apply to vectors and have different results.

#### \+ (operator)

##### With two vectors

* * *

Adds the values from this vector and another vector of the same size. The result is a _new_ vector of the same size which holds the sum.

| Left | Right | Result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Vector2 | Vector2 | new Vector2 |

local a \= vec(1, 2, 3)
local b \= vec(4, 4, 6)
print(a + b, a, b) \-- {5, 6, 9}  {1, 2, 3}  {4, 4, 6}

##### With a vector and number

Operator is commutative and has the same result regardless of order.

The result is a new vector of the same size which holds the result of adding the number to all components of this vector.

| Left | Right | Result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Vector2 | [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) | new Vector2 |
| [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) | Vector2 | new Vector2 |

#### \- (operator)

##### As a unary operator

* * *

Returns a new vector of the same size, but all the components negated. Effectively the same as `[*](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#meta_mul_vn)  -1`.

| Operand | Result |
| --- | --- |
| Vector2 | new Vector2 |

##### With two vectors

* * *

Mathematical definition

Performs [vector subtraction](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Euclidean_vector#Addition_and_subtraction "wikipedia:Euclidean vector")
 with another vector, and creates a new vector from the result.

Subtracts the right vector from the left vector. The result is a _new_ vector of the same size which holds the difference.

| Left | Right | Result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Vector2 | Vector2 | new Vector2 |

local a \= vec(1, 2, 3)
local b \= vec(4, 4, 6)
print(b \- a, a, b) \-- {3, 2, 3}  {1, 2, 3}  {4, 4, 6}

##### With a vector and a number

Only applies when the number is on the **right** side of the operator.

Returns a new vector of the same size containing the result of subtracting the number from all components of the vector.

| Left | Right | Result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Vector2 | [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) | new Vector2 |

##### With a number and a vector

Only applies when the number is on the **left** side of the operator.

Returns a new vector of the same size containing the result of negating all of the components of the vector, then [adding](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#meta_add_vn)
 the number. In effect, `number - vector == (-vector) + number`.

| Left | Right | Result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) | Vector2 | new Vector2 |

#### \* (operator)

##### With two vectors

* * *

Returns a new vector of the same size containing the result of [multiplying](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#multiply)
 the vectors together (component multiplication.)

| Left | Right | Result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Vector2 | Vector2 | new Vector2 |

##### With a vector and a number

Operator is commutative and has the same result regardless of order.

Returns a new vector of the same size containing the result of [scaling](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#scale)
 the vector by the number (scalar multiplication.)

| Left | Right | Result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Vector2 | [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) | new Vector2 |
| [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) | Vector2 | new Vector2 |

##### With a vector and a matrix

This definition only applies if the vector is on the left side. If the vector is on the right side, see [the associated operator on the matrix](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Matrix2#meta_mul_mv "Matrix2")
.

Returns a new vector of the same size containing the result of [transforming](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#transform)
 the vector with the matrix.

| Left | Right | Result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Vector2 | [Matrix2](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vectors_and_Matrices "Vectors and Matrices") | new Vector2 |

#### / (operator)

##### With two vectors

* * *

Returns a new vector of the same size containing the result of [dividing](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#divide)
 the left vector by the right vector (component division.)

| Left | Right | Result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Vector2 | Vector2 | new Vector2 |

##### With a vector and a number

Only applies when the number is on the **right** side of the operator. There is no definition with the number on the left side of the operator.

Returns a new vector of the same size containing the result of [scaling](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector2#scale)
 the vector by the [reciprocal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Multiplicative_inverse "wikipedia:Multiplicative inverse")
 of the number.

| Left | Right | Result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Vector2 | [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) | new Vector2 |

#### \# (operator)

* * *

Returns the number of components in this vector(2)

| Operand | Result |
| --- | --- |
| Vector2 | `2` |

#### < (operator)

* * *

Returns `true` if **all** of the components of the left vector are strictly less than the corresponding components of the right vector.

| Left | Right | Result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Vector2 | Vector2 | [boolean](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |

local A \= vec(1, 2, 3)
local B \= vec(4, 5, 6)
local C \= vec(4, 1, 6)
print(A < B) \-- true
print(B < C) \-- false

#### <= (operator)

* * *

Returns `true` if **all** of the components of the left vector are strictly less than or equal to the corresponding components of the right vector.

| Left | Right | Result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Vector2 | Vector2 | [boolean](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |

local A \= vec(1, 2, 3)
local B \= vec(4, 5, 6)
local C \= vec(1, 2, 3)
print(A <= B) \-- true
print(B <= C) \-- true

#### tostring return value

* * *

Converts the vector to a string representing its components.

| Operand | Result |
| --- | --- |
| Vector2 | [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |

Retrieved from "[https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Vector2&oldid=737](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Vector2&oldid=737)
"

# FiguraMetatables - FiguraMC

This page is a [stub](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "wikipedia:Wikipedia:Stub")
. You can help this wiki by [expanding it](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=FiguraMetatables&action=edit)

figuraMetatables is a table containing the metatables for every type figura adds. Modifying this table will change the metatable on the respective type

"

# ConfigAPI - FiguraMC

(Redirected from [Config](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=Config&redirect=no "Config")
)

This API is only available on the host.

For more information, see [Pings](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Pings "Pings")
.

The `ConfigAPI` allows storing and loading data between reloads of your avatar.

ConfigAPI is available as the global `config` variable.

Data types
----------

Only some data types are supported by ConfigAPI. All other data types will be converted to [nil](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
. The supported data types are:

*   [nil](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
    
*   [boolean](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
    
*   [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
    
*   [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
    
*   [table](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
    
*   all types of [Vector](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector "Vector")
    
*   all types of [Matrix](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vectors_and_Matrices "Vectors and Matrices")
    

ConfigAPI methods
-----------------

### Configuration switching

#### setName

* * *

Also available as `ConfigAPI:name`.

Sets the name of the configuration file to read and write data to. By default, the name of the configuration file is the same as the name of the currently loaded avatar.

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `setName(name [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) )` | self ConfigAPI |

config:setName("my\_config\_file\_name")

#### getName

* * *

Returns the name of the active configuration file.

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `getName()` | [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |

\-- Print the active configuration file's name.
print(config:getName())

### Reading and writing data

#### load

* * *

Read a saved value from the active configuration file (see [setName](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Config#setName)
) by key and return it. If no key is given, return all of the saved keys and values in a table.

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `load(key [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) )` | [any](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)<br> \| [nil](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |
| `load()` | [table](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |

\-- Print all saved values.
printTable(config:load())
\-- Print one specific value.
print(config:load("my\_value"))

#### save

* * *

Associate a key with a value in the active configuration file (see [setName](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Config#setName)
.) Only some types of values are allowed; see [Data Types](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Config#Data_Types)
 for details.

If [nil](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)
 is provided as the value, then the key is removed from the file.

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| `save(key [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) , value [any](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)  \| [nil](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) )` | self ConfigAPI |

\-- Save a value to be used later.
config:save("my\_value", "Figura Wiki")

See also
--------

*   [Persisting Variables](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Tutorials/Persisting-Variables "Tutorials/Persisting-Variables")
     (tutorial)

# FiguraMC:Contributing - FiguraMC

This page is a [stub](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "wikipedia:Wikipedia:Stub")
. You can help this wiki by [expanding it](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=FiguraMC:Contributing&action=edit)

Contributing to the Mod
-----------------------

Please refer to [the GitHub repo](https://github.com/FiguraMC/Figura/blob/1.20/CONTRIBUTING.md)

Contributing to the Wiki
------------------------

### Information

When contributing to this wiki, there are a couple things you should remember:

*   Follow the [rules](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/FiguraMC:Rules "FiguraMC:Rules")
    
*   Follow the [style guide](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/FiguraMC:Contributing/Style_Guide "FiguraMC:Contributing/Style Guide")
    

### Style Guide

_From [Style Guide](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/FiguraMC:Contributing/Style_Guide "FiguraMC:Contributing/Style Guide")
 ([edit](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=FiguraMC:Contributing/Style_Guide&action=edit)
)_

This page is a [stub](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "wikipedia:Wikipedia:Stub")
. You can help this wiki by [expanding it](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=FiguraMC:Contributing&action=edit)

General Guidelines
------------------

*   Avoid writing in the first/second person
    *   Only write in the second person in tutorials
    *   Avoid using [personal pronouns](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/English_personal_pronouns "wikipedia:English personal pronouns")
        
    *   See [Grammatical person](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grammatical_person "wikipedia:Grammatical person")
        
*   Properly capitalize wikilinks where it makes sense

Types
-----

When creating a type page, please use the following layout:

_From [TypeExample](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/FiguraMC:Contributing/Style_Guide/TypeExample "FiguraMC:Contributing/Style Guide/TypeExample")
 ([edit](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=FiguraMC:Contributing/Style_Guide/TypeExample&action=edit)
)_

Page title
==========

(Don't add an additional level-1 heading for the page title.)

If necessary, use `{{[host only](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Template:Host_only "Template:Host only") }}`, `{{[Notice](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Template:Notice "Template:Notice") |...}}`, or `{{[Notice/Warning](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Template:Notice/Warning "Template:Notice/Warning") |...}}`. For example:

This API is only available on the host.

For more information, see [Pings](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Pings "Pings")
.

  
(Important information about the type...)

Methods
-------

| Method | Brief description |
| --- | --- |
| [methodName](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/FiguraMC:Contributing#methodName) | description |
| [secondCategoryMethod](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/FiguraMC:Contributing#secondCategoryMethod) | Returns the position of the ModelPart as a [Vector3](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector3 "Vector3") |

Fields
------

| Field | Description | Type |
| --- | --- | --- |
| field | Brief description | [type](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Type "Type") |
| TICK | Runs every tick the player is rendered | [Event](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Event_(type) "Event (type)") |

Methods
-------

Brief description

#### methodName

* * *

Description

Overloads:

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| pos [Vector3](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector3 "Vector3")<br>, name [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)<br> \| [nil](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) | [nil](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |
| x [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)<br>, y [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)<br>, z [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)<br>, name [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)<br> \| [nil](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) | [nil](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |

Description

\-- Example Code

### Category

Brief description

#### secondCategoryMethod

* * *

Description

Overloads:

| Arguments | Return Type |
| --- | --- |
| rotation [Vector3](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/Vector3 "Vector3") | [nil](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |
| x [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)<br>, y [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1)<br>, z [number](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) | [nil](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |

\-- Example Code

Each method in the table should link to the section more in-depth explaining it.

### Rules

_From [FiguraMC:Rules](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/FiguraMC:Rules "FiguraMC:Rules")
 ([edit](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=FiguraMC:Rules&action=edit)
)_

*   Keep it PG
*   No hateful language
*   Mainspace (no-prefix) pages should be informative content about Figura, community libraries, or addons
*   Example code needs to be tested before publishing!

### (Temporary) Getting an Account

Until account creation is set up, contact one of the wiki admins.

Retrieved from "[https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=FiguraMC:Contributing&oldid=539](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php?title=FiguraMC:Contributing&oldid=539)
"

# parseJson - FiguraMC

| Arguments | Returns |
| --- | --- |
| JSON: [string](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) | [table](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#2.1) |

Deserializes a JSON string into a table.  
See [toJson](https://wiki.figuramc.org/index.php/ToJson "ToJson")
